bugs.error_message: 3-6x drift in bass/mid/treble event counts between consecutive snapshot runs in the analyzer gate
bugs.root_cause: Module-level _stft_cache was keyed by (id(audio), sample_rate). CPython reuses freed memory addresses, so a second fixture analyzed in the same process could collide with a stale cache entry from the first and read back the wrong STFT.
bugs.fix: PR #102 replaced the id()-based key with a content fingerprint (length, sample_rate, ndim, three sampled amplitude values). Audit (PR feat/cache-key-audit) confirmed no other id()-keyed caches in src/ are vulnerable: the two remaining id()-keyed dicts (selector.py, xsq_writer.py) are function-scoped with all keyed objects held alive in a surrounding container, so id() cannot be recycled. Regression test added at tests/unit/test_librosa_bands_cache.py.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling â€” exceptions would propagate uncaught
Code path had no error handling â€” exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.fix: Rewrote 7â†’10 lines (3 removed) | Also: The metric registry SHALL include `placement_cover; `len(set(model_names)) / len(set(layout_model_name

bugs.error_message: Generator silently produced a timing-only (unlit) .xsq â€” 0 model effect placements, render came out fully black
bugs.root_cause: librosa-only analysis (no vamp QM segmenter) yields 0 hierarchy.sections; build_plan derives 0 section_energies -> 0 theme assignments -> 0 placements, with no fallback to a single whole-song section and no warning
bugs.fix: NOT fixed in code yet â€” surfaced via tools/render_panel placement-count guard that warns on 0 placements. Real fix (whole-song-section fallback in build_plan) tracked for a separate generator change.

bugs.error_message: ffmpeg xstack failed (exit 222) when tiling a single image in render_panel panel mode
bugs.root_cause: ffmpeg xstack requires >=2 inputs; a 1-song panel passed a single mid-frame to _tile
bugs.fix: _tile now shutil.copy's the lone frame when n==1 instead of invoking xstack; added tests/unit/test_render_panel.py covering n=1 and n>1

bugs.error_message: ImportError: cannot import name 'MutableSequence' from 'collections' â€” madmom_beats/madmom_downbeats silently absent on Python 3.11
bugs.root_cause: madmom 0.16.1 processors.py does `from collections import MutableSequence`, removed from collections (moved to collections.abc) in Python 3.10. Repo had a numpy-alias shim before madmom import but no collections equivalent, so the import failed and was swallowed as 'madmom unavailable'.
bugs.fix: Mirror the numpy-alias shim: restore collections.MutableSequence from collections.abc before any madmom import, in vamp_runner.py (module top) and capabilities.py (both the .venv-vamp probe string and the in-process detect_capabilities branch). Verified detect_capabilities()['madmom']=True after.

bugs.error_message: madmom_downbeats failed (ValueError): setting an array element with a sequence. The requested array has an inhomogeneous shape after 2 dimensions. The detected shape was (2, 2) + inhomogeneous part.
bugs.root_cause: madmom 0.16.1 features/downbeats.py:287 does np.argmax(np.asarray(results)[:,1]) where results is [(viterbi_path_array, log_prob), ...] (one per beats_per_bar HMM); the path arrays differ in length so np.asarray(results) is ragged. numpy>=1.24 raises ValueError on ragged array creation; pre-1.24 it made an object array, which madmom relies on.
bugs.fix: _patch_downbeats_asarray() in madmom_beat.py replaces only the `np` name inside madmom.features.downbeats with a proxy that delegates to numpy except asarray, which falls back to dtype=object on ValueError (restoring pre-1.24 semantics). Idempotent, module-scoped; called in MadmomDownbeatAlgorithm._run after import. Verified 25 downbeat marks produced.

bugs.error_message: UnicodeEncodeError crash in background analysis thread on Windows (charmap codec cannot encode character)
bugs.root_cause: Windows defaults stdout/stderr to the cp1252 codec unless the console is UTF-8, so print()/click.echo() calls containing unicode glyphs (âœ“/âœ—) used in src/analyzer/orchestrator.py, src/cli_old.py, and src/analyzer/progress.py raise UnicodeEncodeError
bugs.fix: Reconfigured sys.stdout/sys.stderr to UTF-8 (errors=replace) once in the CLI entrypoint main() so all current and future unicode console output is safe, instead of patching each print site individually

bugs.error_message: E: Unable to locate package freeglut3 (docker build of .devcontainer/Dockerfile)
bugs.root_cause: Debian bookworm dropped the freeglut3 runtime package; only the transitional freeglut3-dev and the renamed runtime libglut3.12 remain. The Dockerfile pinned the now-removed name, so any apt-get against current bookworm mirrors fails on a fresh (non-cached) build
bugs.fix: Replaced freeglut3 with libglut3.12 in the xLights headless rendering dependencies apt-get install line

bugs.error_message: QM Vamp Plugins, pYIN, NNLS-Chroma, and BeatRoot all failed to download during devcontainer build (WARN: ... download failed, skipping)
bugs.root_cause: Each wget to code.soundsoftware.ac.uk used --tries=1 with only a 15s timeout, so any transient slowness/rate-limiting from that academic server caused a permanent skip for the rest of that image build (Docker caches the failed layer)
bugs.fix: Bumped timeout to 30s and tries to 3 for all four downloads (matching the previously-passing Vamp Plugin Pack download), and added a VAMP_DOWNLOAD_CACHEBUST build ARG so a retry can force re-running the layer instead of reusing the cached partial-failure result

bugs.error_message: All 5 Vamp plugin downloads from code.soundsoftware.ac.uk fail (DNS resolves but connection times out completely)
bugs.root_cause: code.soundsoftware.ac.uk (the host serving pre-built amd64 Vamp plugin binaries) is permanently offline, not just slow/rate-limited â€” confirmed via curl: DNS resolves to 138.37.95.184 but the TCP connection times out entirely. Investigated the GitHub-hosted Vamp Plugin Pack installer (github.com/vamp-plugins/vamp-plugin-pack) as an alternative but it is a Qt GUI installer app, not a plain archive, with no unattended-install flag found in the binary â€” not a quick swap-in replacement
bugs.fix: Removed the amd64/arm64 branch in the Vamp Plugins RUN step entirely; now always builds all 9 plugins (QM Vamp Plugins, QM DSP, NNLS-Chroma, segmentino, BBC Vamp Plugins, pYIN, BeatRoot, vamp-aubio, Silvet, Tempogram) from source via their upstream GitHub repos (c4dm/*, bbc/*, aubio/*, cannam/*), reusing the from-source path that already existed for arm64. Eliminates any dependency on the dead host

bugs.error_message: make: *** No rule to make target 'constant-q-cpp/libcq.a', needed by 'silvet.so'. Stop. (silvet build failed during devcontainer image build; only plugin missing from /usr/local/lib/vamp after switching to from-source builds)
bugs.root_cause: cannam/silvet's Makefile.linux 'all' target lists the constant-q-cpp submodule and the plugin binary as sibling prerequisites, but the plugin link rule depends on constant-q-cpp/libcq.a as a plain file with no explicit build rule tying it to the submodule's phony target -- so make -j12 races them and can try to link before the submodule finishes building
bugs.fix: Dropped -j12 for the silvet build only (kept parallelism for all other plugins), forcing sequential build order so constant-q-cpp finishes before silvet.so links against it. Verified in an isolated debian:bookworm container before applying

bugs.error_message: Generated .xsq inside the fully-provisioned devcontainer still reports capabilities: {'vamp': False, 'madmom': False} and 'Stem separation failed: .venv-vamp not found', even though vamp/madmom/demucs are all confirmed importable in the container's system python3
bugs.root_cause: devcontainer.json's XLIGHT_VENV_VAMP was set to /workspace/.venv-vamp/bin/python by bug-082's fix (2026-04-22), but the Dockerfile explicitly creates the vamp-capable python symlink at /home/node/.venv-vamp/bin/python -- 'outside the workspace bind mount so it survives container restarts' (Dockerfile comment, line 353). /workspace is bind-mounted from the host repo at runtime, so nothing baked into that path at image-build time would survive anyway. bug-082's fix pointed the env var at the wrong location, silently disabling all vamp/madmom/demucs capability detection for every devcontainer session since
bugs.fix: Reverted XLIGHT_VENV_VAMP to /home/node/.venv-vamp/bin/python, matching where the Dockerfile actually creates the symlink. bug-082 should be treated as superseded/incorrect

bugs.error_message: UI shows "Theme 'bio-lume' not found" when assigning any real theme from the Theme screen's grid
bugs.root_cause: _VALID_THEME_IDS was a hardcoded set of 6 placeholder slugs (shimmer-wash, driving-pulse, peak-flash, solo-chase, bridge-burn, neutral-glow) left over from before the real theme catalog (feature 019/041, 21+ themes) was wired up. The real catalog is loaded and slugified by themes.py:_load_themes(), which the frontend actually uses to populate the grid, so every real theme_id it sends back was rejected
bugs.fix: Replaced the hardcoded set with a live _valid_theme_ids() helper that calls themes.py:_load_themes() and reads real theme_id values, so it can never go stale again

bugs.error_message: review command duplicated: editing src/cli_old.py review_cmd had zero effect on actual xlight-analyze review behavior
bugs.root_cause: src/cli/__init__.py imports the new split modules (analyze, library, review, scoring) BEFORE extras.py re-registers leftover cli_old.py commands, and extras.py only adds a legacy command 'if cmd_name not in cli.commands' -- so src/cli/review.py's own @cli.command('review') always wins and cli_old.py's identically-named review_cmd is dead code, silently never invoked
bugs.fix: Made the intended change (host=0.0.0.0 override via XLIGHT_REVIEW_HOST env var, for running the review server inside Docker with -p port mapping) in src/cli/review.py instead, and reverted the pointless edit in cli_old.py

bugs.error_message: User: 'i click re-analyze and there is no feedback to let me know if it is doing anything' -- backend log confirmed the pipeline was actually re-running (demucs/vamp) but the Analyze screen kept showing the static 'Analysis complete' summary card
bugs.root_cause: The compact 'already analyzed' summary card at Analyze.tsx:482 was gated only on `alreadyAnalyzedAtMount`, a ref computed once at mount and never updated. handleReanalyze() correctly flips the live `analysisComplete` state to false and restarts the SSE-driven analysis, but the render's early-return didn't check `analysisComplete`, so the sticky summary card never yielded to the live progress view (phase strip/detector list/elapsed timer) while re-analysis ran.
bugs.fix: Changed the guard from `if (alreadyAnalyzedAtMount && !error)` to `if (alreadyAnalyzedAtMount && analysisComplete && !error)` so the summary only shows when nothing is in flight.

bugs.error_message: User: 'the generated sequence is basically empty and useless' -- downloaded .xsq only contained bare <section_assignment> XML attributes, no props/effects/timing
bugs.root_cause: _run_export called build_plan(source_file=, sections=, assignments=, layout=) -- kwargs that never matched the real signature build_plan(config: GenerationConfig, hierarchy, props, groups, effect_library, theme_library) (src/generator/plan.py:88). This TypeError'd on every export, 100% of the time, and was swallowed by a bare `except Exception: pass` with no logging anywhere, silently falling back to a hand-rolled stub XML writer (just serialized override values as XML attributes) that was never a real xLights sequence. Separately, the Import Layout endpoint (src/review/api/v1/layout.py::post_layout) parsed the uploaded xlights_rgbeffects.xml in memory for a prop-summary preview and discarded the raw bytes -- so even a correct build_plan() call would have had no real layout_path to parse full model geometry from.
bugs.fix: Wired _run_export to call the existing, tested src.evaluation.generator_runner.run() (orchestrator -> parse_layout -> classify_props/generate_groups -> load effect/variant/theme libraries -> build_plan -> write_xsq) instead of the broken inline call, and made it raise/surface real errors instead of swallowing them. layout.py now persists the uploaded XML to ~/.xlight/layouts/<layout_id>.xml (src/review/storage/paths.py::layout_xml_path) and registers it via src.settings.save_settings so a real layout_path exists. Also added an optional theme_overrides param threaded through generator_runner.run() -> GenerationConfig so the user's per-section Theme-screen picks (session assignments) are honored instead of being silently ignored by the auto theme-selector. Known remaining gap (documented in docs/known-broken-tests.md): per-section brightness/hit_strength/dwell_time/color_shift sliders still have zero wiring into build_plan/effect_placer.

bugs.error_message: xOnset Timeline stuck on 'Loading analysis...' forever after a first-time analysis completes
bugs.root_cause: The non-force analyze flow never POSTs /analyze/commit: only ReanalysisDialog (force path) commits. Backend keeps song status='draft' and GET /analysis returns 409 not_analyzed, while Analyze.tsx optimistically flips the in-memory song to status='analyzed' on the SSE 'done' event. Timeline sees status analyzed + analysis null -> renders the 'Loading analysis...' placeholder with no retry and no error.
bugs.fix: Analyze.tsx now POSTs /analyze/commit (run_id from the POST /analyze response, empty assignment_mapping) when the SSE stream reports overall done, and only flips the UI/parent to 'analyzed' after commit succeeds ('already_committed' 409 treated as success for duplicate SSE streams). Note: ReanalysisDialog was never mounted anywhere, so the force path was equally orphaned â€” commit now happens for all runs started by the Analyze screen.

bugs.error_message: xlight-evaluate gate: analyzer suite FAILs with ~3113 qm_onsets_phase timing drifts (every event index-shifted by one)
bugs.root_cause: Analyzer golden baseline (last snapshotted at d3210a8) predates f5435dc, which rebuilt all 9 Vamp plugins from upstream source after code.soundsoftware.ac.uk went offline. The rebuilt QM onset detector emits one extra early event, shifting every subsequent index past the 50ms tolerance. Pre-existing on main; unrelated to any feature diff (verified: fix/xonset-analyze-commit touches only frontend files).
bugs.fix: NOT YET FIXED. Remediation: re-run 'xlight-evaluate snapshot-analyzer' in the devcontainer on the new plugin stack and commit refreshed baselines as a standalone chore (after confirming main produces the same drift).

bugs.error_message: UnicodeEncodeError: 'charmap' codec can't encode character 'â†’' â€” xlight-evaluate gate crashes printing its summary on Windows
bugs.root_cause: f5435dc reconfigured stdout/stderr to UTF-8 in src/cli/__init__.py:main(), but xlight-evaluate has its own entrypoint (src/cli/evaluate.py:main) that never got the same reconfigure, so click.echo of 'â†’' dies on cp1252 Windows consoles after the gate has fully run.
bugs.fix: NOT YET FIXED. Workaround: PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8. Real fix: apply the same stdout/stderr reconfigure in src/cli/evaluate.py:main().

bugs.error_message: Rendered .xsq has no Lyrics timing track even though the session had 29 synced lyric lines after commit
bugs.root_cause: save_session() rewrote the session file with ONLY {schema_version, sections, assignments}, so the first theme-assignment edit (PUT /assignments) silently discarded the lyrics/detected_sections/ghost_boundaries fields that the analyze-commit path had persisted via save_full_session.
bugs.fix: save_session now merges over the existing session payload instead of replacing it. Regression test: tests/review_storage/test_assignments_json.py::test_save_session_preserves_extra_fields. Affected songs need one re-analyze (or re-commit) to restore lyrics.

bugs.error_message: PUT /assignments 404 theme_not_found for default theme ids; 4 tests in tests/review/test_api_assignments.py failing
bugs.root_cause: _KIND_TO_THEME auto-assigned defaults referencing theme ids (shimmer-wash, peak-flash, driving-pulse, solo-chase, bridge-burn, neutral-glow) that do not exist in the theme catalog â€” same stale-theme-whitelist family as f5435dc. Assignment PUTs validate against the real catalog, so tests (and any user re-selecting a default) got 404s. Tests were additionally broken because _import_and_analyze never committed the run (two-step commit flow, see bug-173).
bugs.fix: _KIND_TO_THEME remapped to real catalog ids (warm-glow/aurora/festive-flash/scanning-beam/inferno/silent-night); SectionStrip THEME_ACCENTS extended (legacy ids kept for old sessions); test helper now commits the run; test theme ids updated.

bugs.error_message: Singing-face/lyric-text placements silently absent from generated .xsq when hierarchy has 0 sections (librosa-only analysis cache)
bugs.root_cause: First implementation attached vocal placements to assignments[0].group_effects, but a 0-section analysis produces no assignments at all (same failure family as bug-159), so the placements were dropped.
bugs.fix: Song-scoped vocal placements moved to SequencePlan.vocal_effects (new field); write_xsq merges them independently of section assignments. Regression test: tests/unit/test_generator/test_xsq_writer.py::TestFacesAndTextEffectSerialization::test_vocal_effects_survive_zero_sections.

bugs.error_message: User: why are Spiral mini-1..4 detected as face props? (also Cane-4)
bugs.root_cause: Opening the xLights Faces dialog on any prop writes a NodeRange <faceInfo> shell with every Mouth-* attribute empty; detection keyed on 'has NodeRange faceInfo' matched these shells.
bugs.fix: parse_layout now requires at least one non-empty Mouth-* node range before a faceInfo counts toward Prop.face_definitions. Test: tests/unit/test_grouper_layout.py::TestFaceDefinitions::test_empty_mouth_shell_excluded.

bugs.error_message: import whisperx fails in xlight-dev devcontainer: AttributeError: module 'torchaudio' has no attribute 'AudioMetaData' â€” phoneme alignment silently skipped, no singing faces
bugs.root_cause: Container had whisperx 3.3.0 + pyannote.audio 3.3.2 against torchaudio 2.11; pyannote 3.x references torchaudio.AudioMetaData (removed in torchaudio 2.9+) in a runtime-evaluated annotation.
bugs.fix: pip install --user --break-system-packages whisperx==3.8.6 pyannote.audio==4.0.7 (versions mirrored from the working Windows .venv), then re-pin numpy<2 (the upgrade dragged numpy to 2.4.6, breaking madmom). pyannote-core/metrics declare numpy>=2 but run fine on 1.26.4. Verified whisperx + madmom import and a full VAD/alignment run in-container.

bugs.error_message: User: 'didnt look like the face effect was placed' â€” rendered .xsq had 0 Faces/Text placements; separate Words/Phonemes timing tracks not usable by xLights
bugs.root_cause: Two causes: (1) the session had no words/phonemes (analyzed before the whisperx fix/server restart), so export had nothing to place; (2) even with marks, the writer emitted separate single-layer Words/Phonemes tracks, but xLights consumes lyrics as ONE 3-layer track (phrases/words/phonemes) â€” Faces takes TimingTrack=<track name>, Text takes LyricTrack='<track> - Words'.
bugs.fix: xsq_writer now emits a 3-layer 'Lyrics' timing element when word+phoneme marks exist (_build_lyric_layers); Faces references TimingTrack=Lyrics, Text references 'Lyrics - Words'. Faces/Text settings replaced with strings copied verbatim from user-verified working effects. Tests: TestLyricLayeredTimingTrack.

bugs.error_message: Corpus recipe alternate effect (Ripple) fired on every chorus â€” Shockwave (the corpus-dominant primary) never appeared in verse/chorus songs
bugs.root_cause: variation_seed is the global section index; in alternating verse/chorus structures choruses are always odd-indexed, so raw seed parity locked the alternate effect in for the whole song
bugs.fix: Halve variation_seed before taking parity ((seed // 2) % 2) so qualifying sections that occupy every other slot alternate per occurrence

bugs.error_message: Snowflake drum accents show only first color: On/Shockwave placements carried 2 palette colors, invalid in xLights
bugs.root_cause: _place_drum_accents always set color_palette=theme.palette[:2] regardless of base effect; On and Shockwave render only palette color 1
bugs.fix: Trim accent palette to 1 color when variant base_effect is in _FIRST_COLOR_ONLY_EFFECTS ({'On','Shockwave'}); Strobe keeps 2

bugs.error_message: Effects on snowflake group hidden by per-model drum-accent effects on the individual snowflake props
bugs.root_cause: _place_drum_accents targets individual radial props as a fallback for layouts with no tier-6 group, but fired even when the props' tier-6 group received placements â€” model-level effects cover the group render in xLights
bugs.fix: Skip props that are members of a tier-6 group with placements in assignment.group_effects for the current section

bugs.error_message: ReferenceError: __GIT_COMMIT__ is not defined â€” 11 Chrome.test.tsx failures under vitest
bugs.root_cause: vite.config.ts injects __GIT_COMMIT__/__BUILD_TIME__ via define at build time, but vitest uses a separate vitest.config.ts that never defined them, so rendering the header stamp crashes in jsdom
bugs.fix: Added define block with test-stub values to vitest.config.ts

bugs.root_cause: git status --porcelain walks the whole working tree and exceeds 5s on the Windows bind mount inside the container; TimeoutExpired in the shared try block discarded the already-resolved commit hash
bugs.fix: Split into two try blocks â€” the dirty check is best-effort and on failure only omits the -dirty suffix

bugs.error_message: test_unrelated_group_no_match failed after adding the cane corpus recipe
bugs.root_cause: The pre-existing 'unrelated group' fixture used 06_PROP_CandyCane, which became a legitimate recipe match once the cane family was added
bugs.fix: Changed the unrelated fixture to 06_PROP_Wreath; canes now have their own matching tests

bugs.error_message: horizontal/vertical recipe alternate (Lightning) would silently never fire
bugs.root_cause: _place_corpus_recipe returns None when the effect is missing from the library, and Lightning was not in the builtin catalog, so alternate-seed sections would fall back to the pool with no error
bugs.fix: Added a Lightning EffectDefinition to builtin_effects.json with the corpus-mined parameter set; lesson: when adding a recipe, verify both effect_name and alt_effect_name exist in the builtin catalog

bugs.error_message: About dialog 'Check for updates' opens https://xlight.example.com/download which is dead
bugs.root_cause: DOWNLOAD_PAGE_URL is a ship-time placeholder from feature 052 (Tauri desktop packaging); no real download page exists yet
bugs.fix: DOWNLOAD_PAGE_URL now points at https://github.com/derwin12/xlight-autosequencer/releases; bundle rebuilt. Optional follow-up: hide the button when app_version=='dev'

bugs.error_message: End-of-song fade never fired on real songs (Raining Tacos render had no 01_BASE_All_FADES element)
bugs.root_cause: The fade trigger used the last section's end_ms as 'song end', but the section builder tiles the entire audio duration — trailing silence is inside the last section, so the tail was always ~0ms
bugs.fix: _audible_end_ms: detect the audible end from the full_mix energy curve (last frame >= 5/100), falling back to section end only when curves are absent

bugs.error_message: Corpus recipe chase placements carried the wrong direction (mined preset overwritten)
bugs.root_cause: _make_placement's _ALTERNATING_DIRECTIONS fallback rewrites any present direction param (E_CHOICE_Chase_Type1 etc.) per instance, silently clobbering the corpus recipes' mined direction presets
bugs.fix: preserve_directions flag on _make_placement, set by _place_corpus_recipe; pool placements keep alternation

bugs.error_message: User: 'i selected the .mp4 as the audio but the video wasnt put on a video effect on the matrix' -- new import-video feature's video_path never reached the actual export pipeline
bugs.root_cause: GenerationConfig.video_path / _place_video_effect were wired all the way through build_plan and xsq_writer, but the live export path (Export.tsx -> POST /songs/<id>/export -> export.py::_run_export -> generator_runner.run -> _run_pipeline) never had a video_path parameter at all, so song['video_path'] from the library record was silently dropped before it ever reached GenerationConfig. Same class of bug as bug-172 (kwarg drift between _run_export and run()'s real signature) but this time the parameter didn't exist on either side.
bugs.fix: added video_path param to generator_runner.run() and _run_pipeline(), threaded into GenerationConfig(video_path=...); export.py._run_export now passes video_path=song.get('video_path'). Extended the bug-172 regression guard (test_export_kwargs_match_generator_run_signature) to include video_path.

bugs.error_message: Generated Single Strand and Ripple/Color Wash/Shimmer/Wave/Liquid effects still carried the same stale E_SLIDER_ parameter keys as the Spirals_Movement bug (this file's bug-192 is a different, unrelated video-wiring bug -- these were diagnosed and fixed once already as 'bug-192/193/194' in a session that worked on a separate branch, fix/spirals-textctrl-movement, but that branch was never merged and has since drifted out of sync with main (predates the video-import feature), so the fix never reached main.
bugs.root_cause: Same root cause as the Spirals_Movement fix: xLights migrated these sliders to text controls (E_TEXTCTRL_) in a recent version, but our catalog/writer/variants were seeded from a pre-migration corpus and still carry the stale E_SLIDER_ key (plus, for float-scaled params, the old 10x-integer magnitude instead of the real float value).
bugs.fix: Re-applied the previously-designed fix cleanly on top of current main (not merged from the stale branch, to avoid reverting the video-import feature): Single Strand Chase_Rotations/Chase_Offset, Ripple_Cycles (both catalog entries sharing the key), ColorWash_Cycles, Shimmer_Cycles, Wave_Speed, and 8 Liquid params (Gravity, GravityAngle, LifeTime, X1, Y1, Direction1, Velocity1, Flow1, SourceSize1) all migrated to E_TEXTCTRL_ with corrected float scaling. Re-added TestMigratedSliderKeysAbsent sweeping writer defaults, all builtin variant files, and the catalog text for every migrated bare name.

bugs.error_message: User asked whether the Ripple effect had been fixed too -- prompted a systematic re-audit of 8 more effects (Morph, Pinwheel, Text, Circles, Meteors, Garlands, Galaxy, Faces) against real xLights source (effectmetadata JSON + Panel/Effect.cpp) to find the full extent of the pre-rewrite-corpus key drift.
bugs.root_cause: Same corpus-drift root cause, three distinct symptoms: (1) missing underscores in catalog storage_name strings (Morph_Start_X1 etc, and Meteors_XOffset/YOffset using the wrong SLIDER/TEXTCTRL prefix); (2) wrong default/min/max values with no key error (Circles_Speed/XC/YC/Bounce, Galaxy_Revolutions off by a divisor-360 scale, Garlands_Spacing below its real min); (3) three effects (Garlands, Galaxy, Faces) had no catalog entry at all despite the writer already emitting defaults for two of them.
bugs.fix: Morph: fixed 4 coordinate storage_name typos + Stagger min. Meteors: XOffset/YOffset -> E_TEXTCTRL_. Garlands: added catalog entry, fixed writer's stale Cycles key + Spacing default. Circles: fixed Speed/XC/YC/Bounce values, deleted phantom Circles_Collide, added 3 missing params. Galaxy: added catalog entry, fixed writer's Revolutions scale (3 -> 1440). Text: fixed Text_Line1->Text and Text_Speed prefix (both had no render-side fallback -- would have silently rendered nothing), fixed Text_Dir/Text_Font choice values. Faces: added catalog entry, fixed LeadFrames widget/category validation, added Fade to writer defaults. Pinwheel: cosmetic 'none'->'None' casing + added Style/Rotation params. One scoped regression test class per effect in test_xsq_writer.py.
